name: Asparagus and Acorn Squash Rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim thick asparagus ends; cut squash in half crosswise.
2. Scoop out and discard squash seeds; slice squash into 1/4-inch rings.
3. Cook the squash rings and asparagus in boiling, salted water until tender. Do not overcook.
4. Drain each vegetable immediately after it is tender.
5. Heat butter in a medium skillet.
6. Add sugar and walnuts to the skillet. Stir over medium heat until the sugar is light brown.
7. Deglaze the pan with lemon juice, cider vinegar, and apple cider vinegar, reducing by half.
8. To serve, layer 3 acorn squash rings on each of 4 plates. Place 4 asparagus spears through the acorn squash.
9. Spoon sauce over the vegetables.
NOTE: To make clarified butter, slowly melt a stick of butter over very low heat. Let the solids settle to the bottom; use the clear, oil-free butter on top.
